description: Fix three concrete bugs in the perfect audit infrastructure: (1) perfect.sh never detects script failures because it waits on the wrong PID, silently reporting success even when audits fail; (2) run-prompt.sh suppresses all claude error output and parallel agents all write to the same /tmp/perfect-findings.md file causing data loss; (3) Docker containers launched by CLI audit scripts leak for 24 hours when perfect.sh is interrupted.

userStories.title: Fix FAILED counter in perfect.sh — it always shows 0 because wait uses the wrong PID
userStories.description: perfect.sh launches each audit script as a pipeline `bash "$f" 2>&1 | ts_prefix "$short" &` and stores `$!` (the PID of ts_prefix, the last command in the pipeline) in PIDS[]. The wait loop then does `wait "$pid"` on ts_prefix, which always exits 0 (it just reads stdin until EOF). The exit code of `bash "$f"` is never observed, so FAILED is never incremented and perfect.sh always prints 'All N scripts completed successfully' even when every audit script fails. Fix by wrapping the pipeline in a subshell that exits with `bash "$f"`'s exit code, so `wait "$pid"` returns the correct status.
acceptanceCriteria: When one or more perfect-*.sh scripts exit non-zero, perfect.sh exits non-zero and prints the failure count
When all scripts exit 0, perfect.sh exits 0 and prints success
The PIDS array stores the PID of a subshell whose exit code equals the exit code of the wrapped `bash "$f"` invocation
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .ralph/perfect.sh (wrap pipeline in subshell that exits with bash "$f"'s exit code)

Context:
The bug is in the script launch loop (lines 66-79) and the wait loop (lines 103-113).

Current broken launch code:
```bash
bash "$f" 2>&1 | ts_prefix "$short" &
PIDS+=($!)
```
`$!` here is the PID of `ts_prefix`, not `bash "$f"`. `wait "$pid"` returns ts_prefix's exit code, which is always 0 (while loop that exits when stdin closes).

Fix: wrap in a subshell that propagates the pipeline's first-command exit code:
```bash
(bash "$f" 2>&1 | ts_prefix "$short"; exit "${PIPESTATUS[0]}") &
PIDS+=($!)
```
Now `$!` is the PID of the subshell. `wait "$pid"` returns the subshell's exit code, which is `${PIPESTATUS[0]}` = the exit code of `bash "$f"`.

The wait loop (lines 103-113) does not need changes — it already does `if wait "$pid"; then ... else FAILED=((FAILED + 1)); fi` which is correct once the PID captures the right process.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the ts_prefix function or how output is formatted
- Do NOT change the NAMES array or cleanup logic
- Do NOT change the signal trap
- `${PIPESTATUS[0]}` is bash-specific and correct here; do not use `$?` (that would capture ts_prefix's exit code)

userStories.title: Fix run-prompt.sh: remove stderr suppression and use per-invocation unique findings file
userStories.description: run-prompt.sh has two independent bugs: (1) `claude ... 2>/dev/null` silently discards all error output from claude — if claude is not installed, has an invalid API key, or crashes, the user sees zero output and (combined with the FAILED counter bug in US-001) a false success. (2) All parallel audit agents are instructed to write to the same hardcoded path `/tmp/perfect-findings.md`. Since perfect.sh launches 8+ agents in parallel, concurrent file writes corrupt each other's findings. Fix both: remove `2>/dev/null` so claude errors are visible, and substitute a per-invocation unique path (using `$$`, the shell's PID) for every occurrence of `/tmp/perfect-findings.md` in the assembled prompt before passing it to claude.
acceptanceCriteria: The claude invocation in run-prompt.sh no longer has `2>/dev/null` — claude errors appear on stderr

After `PROMPT` is fully assembled (perfect-prompt.md prepended + per-script prompt), all occurrences of the string `/tmp/perfect-findings.md` in `$PROMPT` are replaced with `/tmp/perfect-findings-$$.md` before passing to claude
Each concurrent run-prompt.sh invocation uses a different findings file path (one per shell PID)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .ralph/run-prompt.sh (two changes: remove 2>/dev/null, add path substitution)

Context:
Two independent bugs in the same file.

Bug 1 — stderr suppression:
Line 128 (current):
```bash
claude "${CLAUDE_ARGS[@]}" < <(echo "$PROMPT") 2>/dev/null | stream_filter
```
Fix: remove `2>/dev/null`:
```bash
claude "${CLAUDE_ARGS[@]}" < <(echo "$PROMPT") | stream_filter
```
claude's stderr will flow to run-prompt.sh's stderr, which is visible through the calling script's `2>&1` redirect in perfect.sh.

Bug 2 — findings file race condition:
The prompt building block (around lines 61-68) assembles the full prompt by prepending perfect-prompt.md. After this, ALL occurrences of `/tmp/perfect-findings.md` in both perfect-prompt.md and the per-script prompt need to be replaced with a unique per-invocation path.

Add this block AFTER the prompt is fully assembled (after the if block that prepends perfect-prompt.md), BEFORE the claude invocation:
```bash
# Use a per-invocation unique findings file to prevent race conditions when
# multiple audit scripts run in parallel and write to the same file.
FINDINGS_FILE="/tmp/perfect-findings-$$.md"
PROMPT="${PROMPT//\/tmp\/perfect-findings.md/$FINDINGS_FILE}"
```
`$$` is the PID of the current run-prompt.sh shell — unique per invocation.
The bash `${var//pattern/replacement}` substitution replaces ALL occurrences.
Forward slashes in the pattern must be escaped as `\/` in this context.
Forward slashes in `$FINDINGS_FILE` (the replacement) do NOT need escaping.

Constraints:
- Do NOT add a `--print` flag or change how prompts are constructed otherwise
- Do NOT change the stream_filter function
- Do NOT change the argument parsing section
- The substitution must happen AFTER perfect-prompt.md is prepended (so references in the shared guidelines are also substituted) but BEFORE the claude invocation

userStories.title: Add Docker cleanup traps in perfect-cli-*.sh scripts to prevent container leaks on interrupt
userStories.description: perfect-cli-user.sh, perfect-cli-plugin-developer.sh, and perfect-cli-platform-contributor.sh each instruct the Claude agent to `docker run --rm -d` a named container (opentabs-ux-test, opentabs-plugin-dev-test, opentabs-platform-contributor-test respectively). These containers run with `sleep 86400` and stay alive for 24 hours if not explicitly stopped. When perfect.sh is interrupted (Ctrl+C), it kills the claude processes but Docker containers are not children of claude and are unaffected — they leak. Add shell-level EXIT/SIGTERM/SIGINT traps in each script to stop the container on any exit path.
acceptanceCriteria: perfect-cli-user.sh defines a trap that runs `docker kill opentabs-ux-test 2>/dev/null || true` on EXIT, SIGTERM, and SIGINT
perfect-cli-plugin-developer.sh defines a trap that kills `opentabs-plugin-dev-test` on EXIT, SIGTERM, and SIGINT
perfect-cli-platform-contributor.sh defines a trap that kills `opentabs-platform-contributor-test` on EXIT, SIGTERM, and SIGINT
`bash -n .ralph/perfect-cli-user.sh` passes (and same for the other two scripts)
The cleanup trap uses `|| true` so it doesn't fail if the container is already stopped or was never started
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .ralph/perfect-cli-user.sh (add trap for opentabs-ux-test)
- .ralph/perfect-cli-plugin-developer.sh (add trap for opentabs-plugin-dev-test)
- .ralph/perfect-cli-platform-contributor.sh (add trap for opentabs-platform-contributor-test)

Context:
Each script has this structure:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# perfect-cli-XXX.sh — ...
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

read -r -d '' PROMPT <<'PROMPT_EOF' || true
...
PROMPT_EOF

echo "=== perfect-cli-XXX.sh ==="
...
echo "$PROMPT" | bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/run-prompt.sh"
```

The Docker container names in the embedded prompts:
- perfect-cli-user.sh: `opentabs-ux-test` (see `docker run ... --name opentabs-ux-test ...` in the heredoc)
- perfect-cli-plugin-developer.sh: `opentabs-plugin-dev-test`
- perfect-cli-platform-contributor.sh: `opentabs-platform-contributor-test`

Add the trap AFTER the SCRIPT_DIR assignment and BEFORE the heredoc, so it's active for the entire script lifetime:
```bash
# Kill the Docker test container on any exit (normal, error, or signal).
# This prevents container leaks when perfect.sh is interrupted (Ctrl+C).
cleanup_docker() {
  docker kill opentabs-ux-test 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup_docker EXIT SIGTERM SIGINT
```

Use the correct container name for each file (see above).

Constraints:
- The trap must be shell-level code OUTSIDE the heredoc (NOT inside the PROMPT heredoc)
- Use both `EXIT` and signal traps: EXIT fires on normal exit, but signals (from perfect.sh's kill 0) require explicit SIGTERM/SIGINT traps
- `docker rm` is not needed since containers use `--rm` flag (auto-removed on stop)
- Do not change the PROMPT content (the heredoc) — only add the trap before it
- Do not change the `echo | bash run-prompt.sh` invocation at the end
